# Admin Governance Overhaul — Proposal

**Status:** Approved — implemented on `cur/s1b/admin/governance-overhaul` (2026-06-26).  
**Generated by:** Claude Code (admin session 2026-06-26)  
**Scope:** `governance/`, `strategy/`, `AGENTS.md`, `scripts/` — no submodule pointer changes.

Use checkboxes below to approve, modify, or reject each item before handing to Cursor.

---

## 0. Guiding Principles

1. **Rules live in agent config files, not separate governance docs.** If a rule is short enough to state in two sentences, it belongs in `AGENTS.md` or an agent-specific rules file, not a 90-line doc.
2. **Reference docs stay for detail.** Templates, state machines, runbooks — these are looked up, not bootstrapped. Keep them; they don't add to agent context unless pulled explicitly.
3. **current_status.md is admin-only.** Claude Code updates it daily. It is not in any agent's bootstrap path.
4. **Branch ownership is visible.** Prefix encodes the owning agent: `cla/`, `cur/`, `agy/`.
5. **Fewer files to read before working.** Target: executor agents need 3 files before starting a task, not 9.

---

## 1. Three-Tool Model (codify in AGENTS.md)

Replace the current vague orchestration section in `AGENTS.md` with a clear ownership table. This becomes the single source of truth — referenced by `governance/README.md` but not duplicated anywhere else.

| Agent | Prefix | Owns | Does NOT own |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Cursor Pro+** | `cur/` | Sprint execution, all code changes, tests, feature branches, deployments; PR-facing automation (review assignment, merge-readiness, CI triage, branch hygiene) | Governance docs, daily status, planning artifacts |
| **Claude Code** | `cla/` | Governance ops, PR admin, planning artifact edits, research synthesis | Code implementation, feature branches, daily status updates |
| **Antigravity Pro** | `agy/` | Daily status updates; admin automations created natively in Antigravity IDE (prompts + schedules defined there, not in this repo) | Code, governance overrides, strategic decisions |

> **Note:** Antigravity automations are configured directly in the Antigravity IDE — no orchestration scripts or task specs needed in this repo. `orchestrator.py` is archived (see §8); native Cursor multitask/subagents cover local execution.

- [ ] **Approve** three-tool model table as written
- [ ] **Modify:** _______________

## 2. Branch Prefix Convention

**Change:** `cursor/s1b/*` and `agy/s1b/*` → `{agent}/{stage}/{type}/{slug}`

```
cur/s1b/feat/f014-whatever        ← Cursor feature
cur/s1b/fix/login-csrf-edge       ← Cursor bug fix
cur/s1b/chore/api-submodule-sync  ← Cursor chore
cla/s1b/admin/governance-overhaul ← Claude admin
agy/s1b/chore/test-gen-sweep      ← Antigravity automation
```

**Applies to new branches only.** Existing merged branches are historical record; no rename needed.

**Update required in:**
- `governance/branching_guidelines.md` §2 — replace `cursor/<phase-stage>/feat/<feature-name>` examples with new prefix table
- `AGENTS.md` — update branch convention section
- `governance/README.md` — update any branch examples

- [ ] **Approve** `cla/` / `cur/` / `agy/` prefix scheme
- [ ] **Modify:** _______________

---

## 3. AGENTS.md Restructure

Current `AGENTS.md` is 65+ lines of dense prose across "Learned User Preferences" and "Learned Workspace Facts." Problem: agents read it front-to-back and the most critical rules (three-tool model, branch prefix, CPPR cycle) are buried.

**Proposed structure (same file, reorganized):**

```
§0  Three-Tool Model           ← NEW (from §1 of this proposal)
§1  Universal Rules (inline)   ← promoted from agent_context_delivery.md + execution_protocols.md
§2  Branch Conventions         ← updated from §2 of this proposal
§3  Workspace Facts            ← current "Learned Workspace Facts", condensed
§4  Operating Constraints      ← current constraints block, unchanged
§5  Sub-Repo Map               ← current sub-repos table, unchanged
§6  Reading Order (per-agent)  ← NEW simplified reading order (see §5 below)
```

**Rules to promote inline into §1 (currently scattered across 3+ files):**

```
- CPPR / CPPRD / CPPR+D definitions
- One feature at a time on inactive color
- AI must create PRs via gh pr create — post URL in chat
- Plan status values: draft, ready, in_progress, paused, blocked, completed, abandoned, archived
- HitM gate types: required (block), optional (proceed after post), none
- Task/slice naming: T## for tasks, T##.SL# for slices (SL not S)
- READ FIRST: every plan README must list ≤7 files to read before work starts
- DECISION_LOG.md is append-only; check it before any design choice
- runtime_handoff.md hard limit: 200 lines
- No parallel API stacks; no Redis as HTTP version router
- i18n is part of done; deferred coverage must be tracked as a follow-up slice
```

- [ ] **Approve** AGENTS.md restructure
- [ ] **Modify:** _______________

## 4. New CLAUDE.md (Claude Code–specific rules)

Create `CLAUDE.md` at repo root. Loaded automatically by Claude Code on session start. Contains only Claude-specific rules so they don't pollute AGENTS.md for other agents.

**Proposed content (abbreviated):**

```markdown
# CLAUDE.md — Claude Code session rules

## Role
Admin assistance only. No code implementation — that belongs to Cursor.
If asked to implement a feature, write the governance/planning artifact instead and note that Cursor owns execution.
Daily status updates belong to Antigravity — do not run those here.

## Usage constraint
Claude Pro has usage windows. Keep sessions acute. Finish the admin task; don't explore tangents.

## Branch prefix
cla/s1b/{type}/{slug} — admin and governance branches only.

## PR admin
When opening any PR: use `gh pr create` with title and body. Post the PR URL in chat.
Use CPPRD (not CPPR) for governance doc changes: update the relevant CHANGELOG.
```

- [ ] **Approve** creating CLAUDE.md
- [ ] **Modify:** _______________

---

## 5. Simplified Agent Reading Order

**Old:** 9-file sequence (AGENTS.md → glossary → governance README → orchestration → strategic README → strategic context → plan registry → stage README → sub-plan README)

**New — by agent type:**

### Cursor (executor, code work)
```
1. AGENTS.md                                     (rules + workspace facts)
2. governance/plan_registry.md                   (what's active and its status)
3. plans/S1/S1.B/<target-sub-plan>/README.md     (task list for this sprint)
4. READ FIRST files listed in that README        (DECISION_LOG, runtime_handoff, subrepo CHANGELOG)
```
Pull `governance/glossary.md`, `deployment_protocol.md`, `definition_of_done.md` only when the task requires them.

### Claude Code (admin)
```
1. AGENTS.md + CLAUDE.md
2. strategy/current_status.md                    (today's state)
3. governance/plan_registry.md                   (for cross-refs when updating)
```

### Antigravity (automation / new agent, first session)
```
1. AGENTS.md
2. governance/glossary.md
3. governance/plan_registry.md
4. governance/README.md (simplified)
```

- [ ] **Approve** simplified reading orders
- [ ] **Modify:** _______________

## 6. governance/ File Disposition

### Archive (move to `governance/archived/`) — reason: rules now inline in AGENTS.md or superseded by native IDE capabilities

| File | Lines | Reason to archive |
|---|---|---|
| `orchestration.md` | 92 | Navigation map; key facts already in AGENTS.md §3; §3 IDE layer references Cursor/Antigravity skills that no longer need mirroring |
| `skill_roadmap_rollout_planning.md` | 60 | Cursor skill mirror; each IDE now has native skill system; rule substance → AGENTS.md |
| `skill_orchestration_manager.md` | 85 | Same as above |
| `agent_context_delivery.md` | 157 | Rules promoted inline to AGENTS.md §1; doc itself is redundant |
| `sprint_task_specification.md` | 289 | **Cursor-specific.** Move to `.cursor/rules/` or Cursor's context — not universal governance. Retain as Cursor-facing reference, not a governance bootstrap file. |

**Total lines removed from bootstrap read path: ~683**

### Keep as-is (reference docs, only pulled when needed)

| File | Keep reason |
|---|---|
| `plan_registry.md` | Live portfolio source of truth |
| `plan_lifecycle.md` | State machine; Cursor/Claude pulls when transitioning plan status |
| `plan_template.md` | Schema; pulled when authoring new plans |
| `definition_of_done.md` | PWA/i18n/SEO bars; pulled at plan close |
| `deployment_protocol.md` | CPPR+D runbook; Cursor pulls for deploy tasks |
| `execution_protocols.md` | Gate templates; Cursor uses for HitM gate messages |
| `glossary.md` | Vocabulary; first-session reference |
| `runtime_handoff_template.md` | YAML template; copied at plan creation |
| `agent_workspace_isolation.md` | Multi-agent git identity rules |
| `HITM_SCHEDULE_AND_TASKS.md` | Personal ops; gitignored snapshot |

### Simplify

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `governance/README.md` | Trim from ~200 lines to ~60: router table + enums only. Move reading-sequence narrative into AGENTS.md §6. |
| `governance/branching_guidelines.md` | Update §2 branch hierarchy with new prefix table; keep rest as-is |

> **Cursor-side comment:** Keep Cursor-facing references out of universal governance where possible. Cursor-specific sprint specs, PR automation expectations, and subagent guidance should live in `.cursor/rules/` / `.cursor/skills/` or a short `AGENTS.md` section that only tells Cursor where to look. Universal governance should retain source-of-truth contracts, not mirror IDE capability docs.

## 8. scripts/ Cleanup

Four Slack/bridge scripts are deprecated and add noise to the scripts tree. Proposal: move to `scripts/archived/`.

| Script | Reason |
|---|---|
| `scripts/antigravity_slack_runner.py` | Slack bridge; deprecated |
| `scripts/cursor_bridge_daemon.sh` | Cursor headless Slack bridge; deprecated |
| `scripts/cursor_headless_slack_agent.py` | Same |
| `scripts/sprint_slack_pipeline_bridge.py` | Sprint → Slack pipeline; deprecated |

`scripts/orchestrator.py` — archive with the rest of the deprecated orchestration layer unless a future Antigravity-specific need reinstates it. Current Cursor multitask/subagent features make the local wrapper unnecessary for Cursor-owned execution, and admin/other automations should live in Antigravity-native workflows.

> **Cursor-side comment:** Agreed with archiving `orchestrator.py` now. If a future need appears, reinstate it as a deliberately scoped Antigravity adapter, not as a default project coordinator. Also keep PR automation distinct: Cursor may still handle PR creation/readiness loops through `gh` and native subagents, while broader admin automations should remain Antigravity-owned.

- [ ] **Approve** moving 4 Slack scripts to `scripts/archived/`
- [ ] **Approve** archiving `scripts/orchestrator.py`

---

## 9. Changelog Discipline (no file change — just clarify in AGENTS.md)

**Retire the daily-status-report PR pattern.** PRs #57 and #59 were daily status docs committed as PRs — that's now Antigravity's responsibility via `strategy/current_status.md` (no PR needed for daily status). Close any open daily-status PRs without merge.

**CPPRD applies to:**
- Feature merges (subrepo CHANGELOG + any relevant design doc)
- Governance doc changes (parent CHANGELOG entry)

**CPPR applies to:**
- Submodule pointer bumps
- Chore/infra commits with no user-facing change

- [ ] **Approve** changelog clarification in AGENTS.md

---

## 10. What This Does NOT Change

- Submodule pointers — no touch.
- `plans/S1/S1.B/` structure — no touch.
- `strategy/strategic-roadmap-reframe-53be/` — no touch.
- `governance/plan_registry.md`, `plan_lifecycle.md`, `plan_template.md` — no touch.
- VPS deployment or docker-compose — no touch.
- Any subrepo (`finance_manager_api`, `finance_manager_web`, etc.) — no touch.
- `.cursor/` rules and skills — Cursor may update its own rules after reviewing this, but that's Cursor's call.

---

## 11. Implementation Handoff (for Cursor)

**Exception note:** T01–T09 are admin/governance file changes, not sprint code work. Cursor is executing this as a one-time migration on HitM's explicit instruction. Under the steady-state three-tool model these would be Claude or Antigravity tasks — this handoff is the exception that bootstraps that model into place.

Branch prefix is `cur/` because Cursor is the executing agent. Work product ownership shifts to the new model after this PR merges.

```
READ FIRST:
- strategy/admin_overhaul_proposal_2026-06-26.md  (this file — approved items only)
- AGENTS.md (current state)
- governance/README.md (current state)
- governance/branching_guidelines.md (current state)

BRANCH: cur/s1b/admin/governance-overhaul
CYCLE: CPPRD (update parent CHANGELOG.md after merge)

TASKS:
T01: Restructure AGENTS.md per §1, §2, §3, §6 of proposal
T02: Create CLAUDE.md per §4
T03: Archive governance/ files per §6 — move to governance/archived/
T04: Simplify governance/README.md per §6
T05: Update governance/branching_guidelines.md branch prefix per §2
T06: Add superseded note to strategy/cursor_vs_claude_max_cba.md per §7
T07: Add admin-only/Antigravity header to strategy/current_status.md per §7
T08: Move 4 Slack scripts + orchestrator.py to scripts/archived/ per §8
T09: Add CPPRD/changelog discipline note to AGENTS.md per §9

One slice per task. Open single PR to parent repo when all tasks done.
Close open daily-status PRs (#57, #59) without merge as part of PR admin.
```
